title: Ways to Describe Emotions - JEALOUSY
description: Comprehensive guide to understanding jealousy emotions, their triggers, manifestations, and effects

promptingEvents.description: Events that trigger feelings of jealousy
events: An important relationship is threatened or in danger of being lost
A potential competitor pays attention to someone you love
Someone is threatening to take away important things in your life
Someone goes out with the person you like
Someone ignores you while talking to a friend of yours
Someone is more attractive, outgoing, or self-confident than you
You are treated as unimportant by a person you want to be close to
Your partner tells you that he or she desires more time alone
Your partner appears to flirt with someone else
A person you are romantically involved with looks at someone else
You find the person you love is having an affair with someone else

interpretations.description: Interpretations of events that prompt feelings of jealousy
interpretations: Believing that your partner does not care for you any more
Believing that you are nothing to your partner
Believing that your partner is going to leave you
Believing that your partner is behaving inappropriately
Believing that you don't measure up to your peers
Believing that you deserve more than what you are receiving
Believing that no one cares about you
Believing that your rival is possessive and competitive
Believing that your rival is insecure
Believing that your rival is envious

biologicalChanges.description: Biological changes and experiences of jealousy
changes: Wanting to grasp or keep hold of what you have
Wanting to push away or eliminate your rival

behaviors: Violent behavior or threats of violence toward the person threatening to take something away
Attempting to control the freedom of the person you are afraid of losing
Verbal accusations of disloyalty or unfaithfulness
Interrogating the person; demanding accounting of time or activities
Increased or excessive demonstrations of love

effects: Being hypervigilant to threats to your relationships
